summary:
The most reliable method for cleaning blood contamination involves using chlorine-based compounds such as sodium hypochloritethat effectively eliminate a wide range of pathogens including those resistant to other disinfectants like alcohol or quaternary ammonium compounds. This effectiveness has been demonstrated specifically in hospital settings where these chemicals have successfully decontaminated surfaces exposed to viral infections transmitted via blood, making them superior choices over alternatives under real-world conditions involving complex biohazards present in spilled blood. 

Note: While multiple options were considered based on available data—such as alcohols and quaternary ammoniums—they all show significant shortcomings compared to chlorinated solutions regarding performance across various pathogen types encountered during actual spill scenarios involving human fluids rich in proteins and enzymes which can interfere with microbial killing mechanisms.

Final Answer: Option C – Chlorine-based compounds
